test.name: Sweden 1712 — the 30 February anomaly
test.description: Sweden attempted a gradual Julian-to-Gregorian transition starting in 1700, omitting leap days. By 1712 the calendar was a hybrid (1 day off from Julian, 10 days off from Gregorian). To revert to Julian, Sweden added an extra day to February 1712, producing a real, documented date of 30 February 1712. Tests whether Claude recognizes this as a genuine historical date rather than an OCR/transcription error.

input.user_message: I found a Swedish parish record dated '30 February 1712, Stockholm'. Is that an OCR error or could the date really say that?

judge_context: Should recognize 30 February 1712 in Sweden as a REAL, historically attested date — not an OCR error

Should explain the cause: Sweden tried to phase in the Gregorian calendar by skipping leap days starting in 1700, ending up on a hybrid 'Swedish calendar' (1 day ahead of Julian, 10 days behind Gregorian)
Should explain that Sweden reverted to Julian in 1712 by ADDING an extra day to February — so 30 February 1712 (Swedish) really existed
Should give the equivalent in other calendars — Swedish 30 February 1712 = Julian 29 February 1712 = Gregorian 11 March 1712
Should briefly note that Sweden's final Gregorian adoption came later (1753)
